title: Synthesis of a pentathiophene fluorescent probe, 4’,3’’’-bis-carboxymethyl-[2,2’;5’,2’’;5’’,2’’’;5’’’,2’’’‘]quinquethiophene-5,5’
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ea6sf","text":"Poly and oligo-thiophenes have previously been used for in vitro1,2, ex vivo3,4 and in vivo5 imaging of protein aggregates. The probe (p-FTAA) has been developed for the purpose of in vivo staining of protein aggregates such as amyloid deposits. It effectively passes the blood brain barrier and imaging can be performed live with two-photon imaging or ex vivo5.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":56,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":69,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":84,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":353,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":64,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":76,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":91,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":360,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cts7v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xyfhv","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ivwq3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"iso0y","text":"The straightforward synthesis of p-FTAA, including two Suzuki couplings, makes it an attractive probe for studies of most diseases involving protein aggregates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: To a solution at 0 °C with 3-thiopheneacetic acid (1.42 g, 10 mmol) and methanol (20 mL) add acetic acid (1 mL) slowly.
Dilute with toluene and wash with NaHCO3 (sat. aq.) until basic.
Dry with MgSO 4 , filter and concentrate.
Dissolve in 20 mL chloroform/acetic acid (1:1) and cool to 0 °C.
Add 1.78 g NBS (10 mmol)
After five hours dilute with toluene and wash with NaHCO3 (sat. aq.) until basic.
Dry with MgSO 4 , filter and concentrate.
Purify on flush column chromatography (toluene).
Dissolve 2 (2.35 g 10 mmol) and 2,5-thiophenediboronic acid (0.781 g, 4.5 mmol) in 20 mL degassed toluene/methanol (1:1).
Add 4.14 g (30 mmol) K 2 CO 3 and PEPPSI-Ipr (0.204 g, 0.30 mmol) to the solution and heat at 80 °C for 10 minutes.
Filter the reaction mixture on celite, dilute with toluene and wash with NaHCO3 (sat. aq.).
Dry with MgSO 4 , filter and concentrate.
Purify the crude reaction mixture on a reversed phase column (methanol/H 2 O 4:1, 0.05 % trifluoroacetic acid) to give the product.
Add NBS (0.460 g, 2.04 mmol) to a solution of the trimer ( 3 , 0.382 g, 0.973 mmol) in chloroform/acetic acid (1:1, 5 mL, 0 °C).
Stir for 4 hours before dilution with diethyl ether (20 mL)
Wash with NaHCO3 (sat. aq., 20 mL) and finally water (20 mL).
Dry with MgSO 4 , filter and concentrate.
Purify by flash column chromatography (toluene).
Add K 2 CO 3 (4.14 g, 30 mmol), PEPPSI-IPr (0.204 g, 0.30 mmol) and 5-(dihydroxyboryl)-2-thiophenecarboxylic acid to a solution of 4 (5.50 g, 10 mmol) in degassed toluene/methanole (1:1, 30 mL).
Heat at 80 °C for 10 minutes
Add HCl (20 mL, 1 M, aq.) and collect the red precipitate
Dissolve the precipitate in boiling dioxane and filter it on celite followed by precipitation by addition of a small amount of water.
Dry the precipitate and add two equimolar amounts of NaOH (1 M, aq.)
The product is afforded by concentration or freeze drying.
Low yield: Repeat reaction with fresh reagents.
One week

During the cross coupling reactions generating product 5 and 3 both temperature and time should be precisely monitored. If available a microwave reactor can be used. The purification of product 3 should be performed on reversed phase column for best result.
m/z calcd for C 26 H 15 O 8 S 5 : [M-H]- 614.9371; Found 614.9422

Efficient imaging of amyloid deposits in Drosophila models of human amyloidoses by Ina Berg, K Peter R Nilsson, Stefan Thor, and Per Hammarström published in Nature Protocols on 22 Jan, 2010 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nprot.2010.41
Figure 1. *Scheme 1.* Proposed synthetic route to p-FTAA (5)
